summary: Chromium credential stealer targeting Chrome/Brave/Edge on Windows
details: Package ships a productized browser credential stealer for Windows. The main entry loads bytenode-compiled bytecode (index.jsc, src/license.jsc) which detects installed Chromium-based browsers (Chrome, Brave, Edge), force-terminates them via `taskkill /F /IM` to release database locks, copies the `Cookies` and `Login Data` SQLite databases, and uses a shipped native Windows module (build/Release/debugelevator.node) to extract DPAPI master keys and Chrome App-Bound Encryption (v20) keys. It then decrypts cookies and saved passwords and writes them in cleartext (Netscape cookie format for cookies, plaintext for credentials) via routines named `extractMasterKey` and `extractPasswordsToFiles`. SQL queries observed include `SELECT host_key, name, encrypted_value, path, expires_utc, is_secure, is_httponly FROM cookies` and `SELECT origin_url, username_value, password_value FROM logins`. Execution is gated by a license-key allowlist fetched at runtime from a mutable third-party GitHub raw URL (`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tabo1337/SaatPCBKod/refs/heads/main/key.txt`) under an account unrelated to the package publisher, giving the operator runtime control over who can unlock the stealer and the ability to swap in new gating or payloads. The entire logic is shipped as bytenode-compiled bytecode plus an opaque 680KB native binary with no source, and package.json carries placeholder `yourusername` repository/homepage/bugs URLs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
